'''Jason Netherton''' (born 5 October 1982) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who spent most of his career playing for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. His usual position was {{rlp|sr}}.
==Playing career==
Jason Netherton, or 'Nev' was born in Hull, but was signed up by [[Leeds Rhinos]] from local amateur club West Hull. He is noted for his high work rate and tenacious defence, provides Rovers with plenty of the all-important power in the pack.{{Citation needed|date=August 2009}} On 3 June 2011, Jason signed a new deal with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] until the end of the 2013 season.<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/HULL-KR-New-Taylor-deal-imminent/story-12701160-detail/story.html| title=HULL KR: New Taylor deal imminent| newspaper=Hull Daily Mail| date=3 June 2011| accessdate=8 July 2015| archive-date=9 July 2015| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150709205631/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/HULL-KR-New-Taylor-deal-imminent/story-12701160-detail/story.html| url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2013, after 160 [[Super League]] matches, Netherton, now 30, was released from his contract with Hull Kingston Rovers and linked up with the [[Mackay Cutters]] for the 2013 and 2014 seasons of the [[Queensland Cup]]. the Mackay Cutters are the feeder club to [[NRL]] side [[North Queensland Cowboys]].

In the 2014 season, Netherton signed a one-year deal with Hull Kingston Rovers, and appeared sporadically as a {{rlp|sr}}.

==Coaching==
He joined the [[Hull KR]] backroom staff as academy head coach in 2024, and left in December 2025.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Dec 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Hull KR rocked by fresh departure of key member of backroom staff|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-academy-head-jason-netherton-leaves-club}}</ref>
